devanagari: अष्टादशसिद्धिवर्णनम् । श्रीभगवानुवाच । जितेन्द्रियस्य युक्तस्य जितश्वासस्य योगिनः । मयि धारयतश्चेत उपतिष्ठन्ति सिद्धयः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: aṣṭādaśasiddhivarṇanam śrībhagavānuvāca jitendriyasya yuktasya jitaśvāsasya yoginaḥ mayi dhārayataśceta upatiṣṭhanti siddhayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[accusative plural from √ dhṛ ], [ablative single from √ dhṛ ], [genitive single from √ dhṛ ]
[ablative single from √ dhṛ ], [genitive single from √ dhṛ ]
[nominative plural from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sthā class 1 verb]

translation:
The glorious Lord continued:
Super-normal powers (Siddhis) attend upon a yogin who has subdued his senses, is devoted to me, has attained breath-control and has concentrated his mind on me.

devanagari: श्रीउद्धव उवाच । कया धारणया का स्वित्कथं वा सिद्धिरच्युत । कति वा सिद्धयो ब्रूहि योगिनां सिद्धिदो भवान् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīuddhava uvāca kayā dhāraṇayā kā svitkathaṃ vā siddhiracyuta kati vā siddhayo brūhi yogināṃ siddhido bhavān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Uddhava said:
By what (type of) concentration and by what methodology, what kind of super-normal power can be mastered? How many such mystic powers are there, O Acyuta? You are the bestower of such powers to Yogins (and You alone know it).

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । सिद्धयोऽष्टादश प्रोक्ता धारणा योगपारगैः । तासामष्टौ मत्प्रधाना दशैव गुणहेतवः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca siddhayo tāsāmaṣṭau matpradhānā daśaiva guṇahetavaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative dual from √ aś class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ aś class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ aś class 5 verb]

translation:
The Lord explained:
Eighteen kinds of super-normal powers and (the same number of corresponding modes of) concentrations (for attaining these Siddhis) have been proclaimed by those who are thoroughly proficient in Yoga. Out of them, eight are found in their perfection in me[1] (and are found to a lesser extent in those who have attained similarity of form to me) while the remaining ten are acquired by the increasing dominance of sattva-guṇa.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā: They are useful in attaining to me. while the other ten arc conducive to the enjoyment of objects in this world and lead to saṃsāra again.
EK.: They are attainable in their perfection only to those who have established themselves in me but they, being absorbed in me and unaware even of their body, are indifferent to them. The remaining ten manifest and present themselves to a person full of pure sattva.—EB. 15. 38-41.

devanagari: अणिमा महिमा मूर्तेर्लघिमा प्राप्तिरिन्द्रियैः । प्राकाम्यं श्रुतदृष्टेषु शक्तिप्रेरणमीशिता ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: aṇimā mahimā mūrterlaghimā prāptirindriyaiḥ prākāmyaṃ śrutadṛṣṭeṣu śaktipreraṇamīśitā
translation: Atomization, levitation and magnification are the three super-normal powers pertaining to the physical body of the Yogin. The power called prāpii means the ability to act through the senses of others by establishing contacts with deities presiding over those senses (and thus a Yogi can control all beings); Prākāmya is the power to enjoy all things (in the heaven) described in the śāstras and those hidden under the earth as well as objects in the visible world. Īśitā ‘Ruling capacity’ is the ability to use Māyā (and other lower powers) for attaining one’s desire.

devanagari: गुणेष्वसङ्गो वशिता यत्कामस्तदवस्यति । एता मे सिद्धयः सौम्य अष्टावौत्पत्तिका मताः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: guṇeṣvasaṅgo vaśitā yatkāmastadavasyati etā me siddhayaḥ saumya aṣṭāvautpattikā matāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ aś class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ aś class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ aś class 5 verb]
[nominative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ man class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 8 verb]

[nominative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ man class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: Vaśitā (self-control) is non-attachment to objects of sensuous enjoyment[2] and the eighth super normal power is the ability to enjoy fully whatever pleasure one wishes (and hence it is called Kāmāvasāyitā). O gentle Uddhava, these eight powers are considered to be inherent in me[3] (to the highest degree).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Whereby one attains my characteristics—EK.

Footnote [3]:
It is only one of these super-normal powers, that the aspirant after me can hope to get after utmost efforts—EK.

devanagari: अनूर्मिमत्त्वं देहेऽस्मिन्दूरश्रवणदर्शनम् । मनोजवः कामरूपं परकायप्रवेशनम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: anūrmimattvaṃ dehe manojavaḥ kāmarūpaṃ parakāyapraveśanam
translation: Immunity from changes of this body (such as hunger, thirst, grief, delusion, old age and death), ability to see and hear from any distance, power of reaching bodily to one’s desired destination with the speed of the mind, ability to assume any form (at will), capacity to enter the body of any other person (leaving one’s own body in a secure place).

devanagari: स्वच्छन्दमृत्युर्देवानां सहक्रीडानुदर्शनम् । यथासङ्कल्पसंसिद्धिराज्ञाप्रतिहता गतिः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: svacchandamṛtyurdevānāṃ sahakrīḍānudarśanam yathāsaṅkalpasaṃsiddhirājñāpratihatā gatiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ u class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ u class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ u class 5 verb]

translation:
(Ability) to cast off one’s body at will, (capacity) to see (and participate) in the sports of gods with celestial nymphs, the accomplishment of one’s desired objects and ability to exercise authority without any obstruction or insubordinate.
(These ten are the minor mystical powers).

devanagari: त्रिकालज्ञत्वमद्वन्द्वं परचित्ताद्यभिज्ञता । अग्न्यर्काम्बुविषादीनां प्रतिष्टम्भोऽपराजयः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: trikālajñatvamadvandvaṃ paracittādyabhijñatā agnyarkāmbuviṣādīnāṃ pratiṣṭambho
translation: Ability to know the past, present and the future, mastery over and capacity to remain unaffected by the pairs of opposites (like heat and cold, pleasure and pain, etc.), capacity to read the minds of others, the power to neutralise the effects of the fire, the water, the poison and such other things and to suffer no defeat (in any undertaking).

devanagari: एताश्चोद्देशतः प्रोक्ता योगधारणसिद्धयः । यया धारणया या स्याद्यथा वा स्यान्निबोध मे ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: etāścoddeśataḥ proktā yogadhāraṇasiddhayaḥ yayā dhāraṇayā yā syādyathā vā syānnibodha me
translation: These super-normal powers which are accomplished through specific Yogic concentrations have been specially mentioned by me. Now learn from me what type of mystic power is attained by which type of concentration and its procedure.

devanagari: भूतसूक्ष्मात्मनि मयि तन्मात्रं धारयेन्मनः । अणिमानमवाप्नोति तन्मात्रोपासको मम ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: bhūtasūkṣmātmani mayi tanmātraṃ dhārayenmanaḥ aṇimānamavāpnoti tanmātropāsako mama
translation: He who concentrates his mind (which also is subtle like an atom) on me as abiding in the subtle form of elements (tanmātrās) and adores me as a subtle element attains the power of atomizing his body.

devanagari: महत्तत्त्वात्मनि मयि यथासंस्थं मनो दधत् । महिमानमवाप्नोति भूतानां च पृथक्पृथक् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: mahattattvātmani mayi yathāsaṃsthaṃ mano dadhat mahimānamavāpnoti bhūtānāṃ ca pṛthakpṛthak
translation: Focusing his mind on me and contemplating me, the all-pervading Supreme Soul as ensouling the principle called Mahat and extending the mind to that dimension, attains the super-normal power of ‘magnification’ and concentrating the mind on me as abiding in any of the bhūtas (gross elements) he possesses the all-pervasiveness of that particular element.

devanagari: परमाणुमये चित्तं भूतानां मयि रञ्जयन् । कालसूक्ष्मार्थतां योगी लघिमानमवाप्नुयात् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: paramāṇumaye cittaṃ bhūtānāṃ mayi rañjayan kālasūkṣmārthatāṃ yogī laghimānamavāpnuyāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rañj ], [vocative single from √ rañj ]
[imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: [4] Having (concentratedly) devoted his mind on me as abiding in the atoms of the gross elements, (and meditating upon me as the subtlest of subtle division of Time) the Yogin attains the power of subtleness.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Padaratnāvalī Attaching his heart on me as the pervader of the most subtle division of time and the subtle-most atoms of the elements the login attains the power of subtleness.

devanagari: धारयन्मय्यहंतत्त्वे मनो वैकारिकेऽखिलम् । सर्वेन्द्रियाणामात्मत्वं प्राप्तिं प्राप्नोति मन्मनाः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: dhārayanmayyahaṃtattve mano vaikārike sarvendriyāṇāmātmatvaṃ prāptiṃ prāpnoti manmanāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ dhṛ ], [vocative single from √ dhṛ ]
[nominative single from √ dhṛ ], [vocative single from √ dhṛ ], [accusative single from √ dhṛ ]

translation: Having thoroughly concentrated his mind on me wholly as abiding in the Sāttvika type of Ego (Ahaṃkāra) and regarding me as the indwelling Soul of all the sense-organs, the yogin attains the mystic power called Prāpti (enabling him to function through the senses of others).

devanagari: महत्यात्मनि यः सूत्रे धारयेन्मयि मानसम् । प्राकाम्यं पारमेष्ठ्यं मे विन्दतेऽव्यक्तजन्मनः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: mahatyātmani yaḥ sūtre dhārayenmayi mānasam prākāmyaṃ pārameṣṭhyaṃ me vindate
translation: He who establishes his mind on me as abiding in the Sūtra (in its all-contacting aspect)[5] attains the mystic power of the highest order called Prākāśya[6] possessed by me as ensouling me in the Mahat tattva which is born of the Avyakta.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā explains that although the Mahat is mentioned in the previous verse (No. 11) there the emphasis is on its physical aspects while here the Sūtra aspect of the Mahat connecting it with all things, is implied. Padaratnāvalī interprets that the Sūtra is the chief vital breath (mukhya prāṇa) which pervades the Mahat.

Footnote [6]:
According to Padaratnāvalī Prākāśya is the supreme capacity to know the Vedas and everything else (sarva-vedādi-jñāna-śraiṣṭhyāpādakam prākāśyam) and it is obtained through the favour of Prāṇa, Bhāratī and Mahāviṣṇu:
sarve guṇās tu prāṇasya paramātma-prasādataḥ /
prāṇa-viṣṇoḥ prasādena bhāratyāḥ samprakīrtitāḥ //
EK. states that with this Supernormal power (when gifted by the Lord) the yogi is capable of creating crores of Brahmāṇḍas (EB. 15.85-86) But Bhāvāratha Dīpikā specifically asserts that the yogi never gets the power of creating the universe:
na tu viśva-sṛṣtyādi-kartṛtva-lakṣaṇam /

devanagari: विष्णौ त्र्यधीश्वरे चित्तं धारयेत्कालविग्रहे । स ईशित्वमवाप्नोति क्षेत्रज्ञक्षेत्रचोदनाम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: viṣṇau tryadhīśvare cittaṃ dhārayetkālavigrahe sa īśitvamavāpnoti kṣetrajñakṣetracodanām
translation: He who concentrates his mind on me as Viṣṇu (the pervaḍer of universe), the Controller of the Māyā consisting of three guṇas and the Inḍwcller of Time attains the mystic power of impelling all bodies and the individual souls.

devanagari: नारायणे तुरीयाख्ये भगवच्छब्दशब्दिते । मनो मय्यादधद्योगी मद्धर्मा वशितामियात् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: nārāyaṇe turīyākhye bhagavacchabdaśabdite mano mayyādadhadyogī maddharmā vaśitāmiyāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ śabd class 10 verb], [vocative dual from √ śabd class 10 verb], [accusative dual from √ śabd class 10 verb], [locative single from √ śabd class 10 verb]
[nominative dual from √ śabd class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ śabd class 10 verb], [vocative dual from √ śabd class 10 verb], [accusative dual from √ śabd class 10 verb]

translation: The Yogin who focuses his mind on me as Nārāyaṇa, the Fourth Principle,[7] one specifically designated by the term ‘Bhagavān’[8] attains the same characteristics as mine and becomes the master of power called Vaśitā (absolute non-attachment to everything though he controls them all).
purport:
Footnote [7]:
There have been different opinions about the previous three stages transcended before attaining the fourth: The following are the 1st three stages:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā: (1) Virāṭ (2) Hiraṇyagarbha and (3) Kāraṇa
EK.: (1) Jīva (2) Śiva and (3) Prakṛti.
(2) Wakefulness (jāgratī), dream state (svapna) and deep sleep (Suṣupti)

Footnote [8]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā quotes the famous verse which enumerates the six excellences of the Lord, viz. absolute mastery over the universe, perfect righteousness, highest glory, afluence, perfect knowledge and complete dispassion.
aiśvaryasya samagrasya, dharmasya, yaśasaḥ śrīyaḥ /
jñāna-vairāgyayoścaiva ṣaṇṇāṃ bhaga itīraṇā //

devanagari: निर्गुणे ब्रह्मणि मयि धारयन्विशदं मनः । परमानन्दमाप्नोति यत्र कामोऽवसीयते ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: nirguṇe brahmaṇi mayi dhārayanviśadaṃ manaḥ paramānandamāpnoti yatra kāmo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ dhṛ ], [vocative single from √ dhṛ ]

translation: The Yogī who concentrates his pure mind on me as the attributeless Brahman, attains Supreme Spiritual joy and bliss when all desires cease altogether (Hence it is called Kāmāvasāyitā).

devanagari: श्वेतद्वीपपतौ चित्तं शुद्धे धर्ममये मयि । धारयञ्छ्वेततां याति षडूर्मिरहितो नरः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: śvetadvīpapatau cittaṃ śuddhe dharmamaye mayi dhārayañchvetatāṃ yāti ṣaḍūrmirahito naraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [locative single from √ śudh class 1 verb], [locative single from √ śudh class 4 verb]

[nominative dual from √ śudh class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ śudh class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ śudh class 1 verb], [locative single from √ śudh class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ śudh class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ śudh class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ śudh class 4 verb], [locative single from √ śudh class 4 verb]

[nominative dual from √ śudh class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ śudh class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ śudh class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ śudh class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ śudh class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ śudh class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ śudh class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ śudh class 4 verb]
[nominative single from √ dhṛ ], [vocative single from √ dhṛ ]

translation: He who fixes his mind on me, the Lord of Śveta- dvīpa, who am absolutely pure (uninfluenced by Rajas, and Tamas), and the very Dharma incarnate, attains whiteness (i.e. absolute purity untainted by guṇas), and becomes free from the six waves or changes (viz. hunger and thirst, old age and death, delusion and grief which affect all beings).

devanagari: मय्याकाशात्मनि प्राणे मनसा घोषमुद्वहन् । तत्रोपलब्धा भूतानां हंसो वाचः शृणोत्यसौ ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: mayyākāśātmani prāṇe manasā ghoṣamudvahan tatropalabdhā bhūtānāṃ haṃso vācaḥ śṛṇotyasau
translation: This swan (i.e. the individual soul) who mentally meditates on the (ever-abiding “unstruck” spiritual) sound in me who am the Soul of the sky (and the cosmic vital breath as well), attains the supernormal power of hearing (from any distance), the sounds of all beings (as all words or utterances repose in the sky.)

devanagari: चक्षुस्त्वष्टरि संयोज्य त्वष्टारमपि चक्षुषि । मां तत्र मनसा ध्यायन्विश्वं पश्यति दूरतः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: cakṣustvaṣṭari saṃyojya tvaṣṭāramapi cakṣuṣi māṃ tatra manasā dhyāyanviśvaṃ paśyati dūrataḥ
translation: With his eye-sight united with the Sun and the Sun with eye and meditating on me in the solar-ocular union becomes endowed with a subtle vision and can view everything in the universe (This is the dūra-darśana siddhi).

devanagari: मनो मयि सुसंयोज्य देहं तदनुवायुना । मद्धारणानुभावेन तत्रात्मा यत्र वै मनः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: mano mayi susaṃyojya dehaṃ tadanuvāyunā maddhāraṇānubhāvena tatrātmā yatra vai manaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: [9] When the mind and the body along with the vital breath, are properly established in me, (one attains the miraculous power called mano-java and) due to the efficacy of this method of meditation. The body is transported (with the speed of the mind) wherever the mind goes (This is the “dūragamana-siddhi”—potency to go anywhere at will).
purport:
Footnote [9]:
When, in contemplation, die mind and vital breath along with the body arc correctly established in me—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: यदा मन उपादाय यद्यद्रूपं बुभूषति । तत्तद्भवेन्मनोरूपं मद्योगबलमाश्रयः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā mana upādāya yadyadrūpaṃ bubhūṣati tattadbhavenmanorūpaṃ madyogabalamāśrayaḥ
translation: When a Yogī utilises his mind as the material cause and desires to assume whatever form he likes, he can transform himself that way at his sweet will, as he depends on the power of contemplating on me (who am of inconceivable potency and of innumerable forms).

devanagari: परकायं विशन्सिद्ध आत्मानं तत्र भावयेत् । पिण्डं हित्वा विशेत्प्राणो वायुभूतः षडङ्घ्रिवत् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: parakāyaṃ viśansiddha ātmānaṃ tatra bhāvayet piṇḍaṃ hitvā viśetprāṇo vāyubhūtaḥ ṣaḍaṅghrivat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ viś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ viś class 6 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sidh class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ sidh class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ sidh class 4 verb], [locative single from √ sidh class 4 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sidh class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ sidh class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ sidh class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ sidh class 4 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sidh class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ sidh class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ sidh class 1 verb], [locative single from √ sidh class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sidh class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sidh class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ sidh class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ sidh class 1 verb]

translation: If a person accomplished in the power of entering another person’s body has perfectly controlled his vital airs and senses desires to enter the body of another person, he should contemplate that he has occupied that body. Leaving his own (previous) body (along with the chief vital air—prāṇa and the astral body he should enter the external cosmic air and thus enter in the form of air (through the nostrils of that body) into the new body as a six-legged black-bee does (from one flower to another). (This power is known as para-kāyā- praveśa (entrance into another person’s body).

devanagari: पार्ष्ण्यापीड्य गुदं प्राणं हृदुरःकण्ठमूर्धसु । आरोप्य ब्रह्मरन्ध्रेण ब्रह्म नीत्वोत्सृजेत्तनुम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: pārṣṇyāpīḍya gudaṃ prāṇaṃ hṛduraḥkaṇṭhamūrdhasu āropya brahmarandhreṇa brahma nītvotsṛjettanum
translation: (Controlling the downward movement of the vital breath) by blocking the rectum with one’s heels and pushing up (by slow degrees), the vital air (through suṣumnā nāḍī) through the heart, chest, throat and the crown of the head, he should then propel it through the aperture in the crown of the head (which is called Brahmarandhra) directing it to its destination—The parabrahman (or any other state he wishes to have)—and cast off his physical body.

devanagari: विहरिष्यन्सुराक्रीडे मत्स्थं सत्त्वं विभावयेत् । विमानेनोपतिष्ठन्ति सत्त्ववृत्तीः सुरस्त्रियः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: vihariṣyansurākrīḍe matsthaṃ sattvaṃ vibhāvayet vimānenopatiṣṭhanti sattvavṛttīḥ surastriyaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ riṣ class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ riṣ class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sthā class 1 verb]

translation: If a Yogi is desirous to enjoy sportive life in heaven, he should contemplate on the pure Sattva in me. And celestial damsels of sātlvika nature attend upon him with their celestial cars.

devanagari: यथा सङ्कल्पयेद्बुद्ध्या यदा वा मत्परः पुमान् । मयि सत्ये मनो युञ्जंस्तथा तत्समुपाश्नुते ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā saṅkalpayedbuddhyā yadā vā matparaḥ pumān mayi satye mano yuñjaṃstathā tatsamupāśnute

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [vocative single from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation: A person who establishes his mind in me who am reality and is absolutely devoted to me, attains and enjoys the objects of his desires to have al a particular time and in the specific manner, according to his will.

devanagari: यो वै मद्भावमापन्न ईशितुर्वशितुः पुमान् । कुतश्चिन्न विहन्येत तस्य चाज्ञा यथा मम ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: yo vai madbhāvamāpanna īśiturvaśituḥ pumān kutaścinna vihanyeta tasya cājñā yathā mama

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: [10] He who is established in my state who am the ordainer of all and quite independent, shall never meet reverses anywhere and his order will be like my commandment (which can never be thwarted). (This is the potency of inviolable commandment—apratihatājñā.
purport:
Footnote [10]:
He who has devoutly and with firm faith meditated upon me as the Supreme Commander and Ruler of all, will never have his order thwarted from any quarter.

devanagari: मद्भक्त्या शुद्धसत्त्वस्य योगिनो धारणाविदः । तस्य त्रैकालिकी बुद्धिर्जन्ममृत्यूपबृंहिता ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: madbhaktyā śuddhasattvasya yogino dhāraṇāvidaḥ tasya traikālikī buddhirjanmamṛtyūpabṛṃhitā
translation: The yogin whose mind has become (absolutely) pure by devotion to me and who is conversant with the way of concentrating his mind on me (who am the creator, protector and destroyer of the Universe), attains the knowledge of the past, present and future along with that of the birth and the death (of himself and the world as well as the mind of others).

devanagari: अग्न्यादिभिर्न हन्येत मुनेर्योगमयं वपुः । मद्योगशान्तचित्तस्य यादसामुदकं यथा ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: agnyādibhirna hanyeta muneryogamayaṃ vapuḥ madyogaśāntacittasya yādasāmudakaṃ yathā
translation: The sage whose mind reposes in contemplation on me (who am above the pair of opposites like heat and cold), gets his physical body seasoned with yoga and is not adversely affected by fire and other (troubles from water, wind, cold, arms, etc.), just as aquatic animals are not affected by water. (This power is called advandvatā or ‘transcendence over opposites’ which leads to avaṣṭambha siddhi or power of neutralisation of the harmful forces).

devanagari: मद्विभूतीरभिध्यायन्श्रीवत्सास्त्रविभूषिताः । ध्वजातपत्रव्यजनैः स भवेदपराजितः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: madvibhūtīrabhidhyāyanśrīvatsāstravibhūṣitāḥ dhvajātapatravyajanaiḥ sa bhavedaparājitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ ru class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ ru class 2 verb]
[vocative single from √ ru class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ ru class 2 verb]

translation: He who meditates (with a concentrated mind) on the various forms and incarnations of mine (exhibiting various powers and) adorned with the śrī-vatsa mark and my missiles (like Sudarśana, etc.) and accompanied with my banner (with the emblem of Garuḍa), umbrella and chowries, would never be vanquished.

devanagari: उपासकस्य मामेवं योगधारणया मुनेः । सिद्धयः पूर्वकथिता उपतिष्ठन्त्यशेषतः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: upāsakasya māmevaṃ yogadhāraṇayā muneḥ siddhayaḥ pūrvakathitā upatiṣṭhantyaśeṣataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kath class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ kath class 10 verb]
[nominative plural from √ kath class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ kath class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ kath class 10 verb]

[nominative dual from √ sthā class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sthā class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]

translation: All the supernormal powers detailed above attend upon the sage who propitiates me according to the appropriate methods of concentration (described in the above-mentioned verses).

devanagari: जितेन्द्रियस्य दान्तस्य जितश्वासात्मनो मुनेः । मद्धारणां धारयतः का सा सिद्धिः सुदुर्लभा ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: jitendriyasya dāntasya jitaśvāsātmano muneḥ maddhāraṇāṃ dhārayataḥ kā sā siddhiḥ sudurlabhā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ dhṛ ], [ablative single from √ dhṛ ], [genitive single from √ dhṛ ]
[ablative single from √ dhṛ ], [genitive single from √ dhṛ ]

translation: What super-normal power can be difficult to accomplish in the case of a sage who, having controlled his senses, subdued his mind and mastered breath-control, firmly concentrates his mind on me?

devanagari: अन्तरायान्वदन्त्येता युञ्जतो योगमुत्तमम् । मया सम्पद्यमानस्य कालक्षपणहेतवः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: antarāyānvadantyetā yuñjato yogamuttamam mayā sampadyamānasya kālakṣapaṇahetavaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vad class 1 verb]
[accusative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb], [ablative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [genitive single from √ yuj class 7 verb]
[ablative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [genitive single from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation: But in the case of persons who practise the excellent course of yoga in order to realize me through my grace, the wise call these siddhis as impediments, as they involve waste of time (in God-realization).

devanagari: जन्मौषधितपोमन्त्रैर्यावतीरिह सिद्धयः । योगेनाप्नोति ताः सर्वा नान्यैर्योगगतिं व्रजेत् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: janmauṣadhitapomantrairyāvatīriha siddhayaḥ yogenāpnoti tāḥ sarvā nānyairyogagatiṃ vrajet
translation: Whatever super-normal powers are accomplished through birth (e.g. power of aviation to birds or gods), medicinal plants, penance, and utterance of mantras,[11] are all obtained through yoga, but one cannot attain the highest fruit of yoga (viz. emancipation from saṃsāra) through these other means.

devanagari: सर्वासामपि सिद्धीनां हेतुः पतिरहं प्रभुः । अहं योगस्य साङ्ख्यस्य धर्मस्य ब्रह्मवादिनाम् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvāsāmapi siddhīnāṃ hetuḥ patirahaṃ prabhuḥ ahaṃ yogasya sāṅkhyasya dharmasya brahmavādinām
translation: Being the source and the Lord of all these supernormal powers, I am their controller. Equally I am the master of yoga (paths of union with God), of Sāṅkhya (the path of knowledge leading God-realization), of Dharma (righteous duties as ordained by the Vedas), and even of the exponents of Brahma (or the Veda).

devanagari: अहमात्मान्तरो बाह्योऽनावृतः सर्वदेहिनाम् । यथा भूतानि भूतेषु बहिरन्तः स्वयं तथा ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: ahamātmāntaro bāhyo yathā bhūtāni bhūteṣu bahirantaḥ svayaṃ tathā
translation:
I am the Soul of all embodied beings, their Inner Controller who pervades them (even externally), though I myself am unenclosed. Just as the (five) elements permeate living beings in the world from inside and outside, so I pervade them all and still retain my independent existence.
[back to top]
